preamble: The transformation from a voiceless palatal plosive (c) to a voiceless alveolar lateral fricative (ɬ) represents a complex phonetic shift involving lenition, a change in the place of articulation, and the acquisition of laterality. This change involves the stop articulation becoming a fricative (lenition), the point of articulation moving from the palate to the alveolar ridge, and the airflow becoming lateral rather than central.
phoneticEffects: Lenition (plosive to fricative), change in place of articulation (palatal to alveolar), lateralization

examples.note: Proto-Bantu *c regularly developed into /ɬ/ in Nguni languages.

sources: Marten, L., & Kula, N. C. (2025). Inheritance and Contact in the Development of Lateral Obstruents in Nguni Languages. MDPI.
